title: gnaphalium ericifolium
intro: Gnaphalium ericifolium, also known as Sea Ayre or Sea Cotton, is a perennial flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to Europe, western Asia, and northwest Africa, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues and digestive problems. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in herbal preparations to help alleviate symptoms such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as to soothe digestive issues like ulcers and diarrhea. In herbal medicine, Gnaphalium ericifolium is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is said to offer various health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved respiratory function.
